Route builder — order, dates, book

Pick your start date, tick places and set nights for each — check-in and check-out dates are computed by chaining from the start date. Edit the # number to reorder: type 2 to slot a place into position 2, everything after shifts down and keeps its nights. The trip always ends back at Las Vegas. The trip map above redraws your route as you go.

#PlaceNightsCheck-inCheck-outKmHWeatherBook

Km are driving distances between consecutive stops; "~" legs are estimates. Hours assume ~95 km/h trip average incl. short breaks. Rows marked alternative are researched options near the route — hover for why, tick to swap them in. Weather is the stay's high/low from Open-Meteo; "≈" means last year's same dates (too far out for a forecast). Booking/Airbnb links open pre-filled searches — availability and prices are theirs.

The places — profiles

Every stop in one gallery. Places on your route in the builder above float to the top, numbered in visit order. Ticket links go to official operators.

Boulder City

stop · US

A historic, gaming-free oasis built for Hoover Dam workers, offering 1930s Art Deco charm, bighorn sheep sightings, and a gateway to outdoor adventure.

Stay: $100-150/n historic downtown — cheaper near Lake Mead $70-100

Try: Pit-smoked brisket, classic ribeye steaks, craft beers, date shakes

  • Hoover Dam Powerplant Tour ~$15 · Powerplant tickets can be pre-booked online, but the full Guided Dam Tour is only sold in-person on a first-come basis.
  • Nevada Southern Railway ~$10 · Excursion trains run primarily on weekends; check seasonal schedules before visiting.
  • Black Canyon River Rafting ~$125 · Highly recommended to book weeks in advance as launches from the dam base are strictly limited.
  • Where to eat
  • The Dillinger — Gourmet craft burgers named after famous gangsters, served in a historic former bank building, ~$15-25/pp.
  • Fox Smokehouse BBQ — Award-winning championship pit BBQ featuring incredible burnt ends and pulled pork, ~$15-30/pp.
  • Milo's Cellar & Inn — Cozy, upscale bistro offering excellent steaks, flatbreads, and a massive wine selection, ~$30-50/pp.
  • In & around
  • Hoover Dam — 15 min drive, marvel at the massive concrete arch-gravity dam and walk across the bypass bridge.
  • Valley of Fire State Park — 1 hour drive, stunning red sandstone formations and ancient petroglyphs.
  • Lake Mead National Recreation Area — 10 min drive, perfect for boating, scenic drives, and desert hiking.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Boulder City is one of only two cities in Nevada that completely bans gambling; do not expect to find slot machines or casinos here.
  • Parking at Hoover Dam's main garage costs $10, but free parking is available in lots further up on the Arizona side.
  • Summer temperatures regularly exceed 100°F (38°C); carry double the water you think you need for any outdoor activity.
  • Interesting facts
  • Built in 1931 by the federal government to house thousands of workers constructing the Hoover Dam.
  • It is a fully planned community designed by landscape architect Saco Rienk DeBoer with a distinct greenbelt layout.
  • Desert bighorn sheep frequently gather at Hemenway Park, grazing on the green grass right next to visitors.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Seligman

stop · US

Known as the Birthplace of Historic Route 66, Seligman is a quirky, neon-lit time capsule of retro Americana, vintage cars, and classic roadside diners.

Stay: $80-130/n historic Route 66 motels — cheaper chain hotels in Kingman $60-100

Try: Classic American milkshakes, Route 66 burgers, slow-smoked BBQ ribs

  • Grand Canyon National Park Vehicle Pass ~$35 · Valid for 7 days, covers all passengers in one private vehicle.
  • Grand Canyon Railway ~$70-230 · Departs from nearby Williams; book well in advance for historic steam train rides.
  • Where to eat
  • Delgadillo's Snow Cap — Famous Route 66 drive-in serving classic cheeseburgers and malts with a side of playful pranks from the staff (~$10-15/pp).
  • Roadkill Cafe / OK Saloon — Steaks, buffalo burgers, and hearty BBQ ribs in a taxidermy-filled, Old West saloon atmosphere (~$20-35/pp).
  • Westside Lilo's Cafe — Huge portions of German-American comfort food, famous for its giant cinnamon rolls and chicken fried steak (~$15-25/pp).
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon Caverns — 25 min drive, underground cave tours and a retro diner 200 feet below ground.
  • Hackberry General Store — 50 min drive, iconic Route 66 museum and photo spot packed with vintage gas pumps.
  • Williams, AZ — 40 min drive, historic gateway town to the Grand Canyon with a bustling downtown.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Many local shops and diners close early (around 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM), so plan dinner early.
  • Cell service can be spotty along the desert stretches of Route 66; download offline maps before driving.
  • Keep your gas tank at least half full, as services are sparse between Seligman and Kingman.
  • Interesting facts
  • Seligman was the main inspiration for the fictional town of Radiator Springs in Pixar's movie 'Cars'.
  • Angel Delgadillo, a local barber, successfully campaigned to establish Route 66 as a historic highway, saving the town from decay.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Williams

stop · US

Known as the 'Gateway to the Grand Canyon,' Williams is a vibrant Route 66 town packed with neon signs, retro diners, and historic steam trains.

Stay: €110-200/n historic downtown — cheaper near I-40 motels €60-100

Try: Smoked beef brisket, ribeye steak, craft beers from local microbreweries, classic Route 66 burgers

  • Grand Canyon Railway ~€85 · Book well in advance, especially for first-class dome cars or seasonal themed rides.
  • Bearizona Wildlife Park ~€35 · Drive-through safari; go early in the morning when the bears and wolves are most active.
  • Where to eat
  • Rod's Steak House — Historic Route 66 steakhouse operating since 1946; famous for prime rib and T-bone steaks, ~€30-50/pp.
  • Cruiser's Route 66 Cafe — Retro diner featuring live music and an outdoor patio; try the slow-smoked BBQ ribs or classic burgers, ~€20-35/pp.
  • Pine Country Restaurant — A local staple famous for its massive selection of over 50 homemade gourmet pies and hearty comfort food, ~€15-25/pp.
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon National Park (South Rim) — 60 min drive, iconic vistas and hiking trails.
  • Flagstaff — 35 min drive, historic downtown, Lowell Observatory, and mountain views.
  • Sycamore Canyon — 40 min drive, rugged wilderness hikes and dramatic red rock cliffs.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Train horn noise: The Grand Canyon Railway runs right through town; light sleepers should pack earplugs if staying nearby.
  • Altitude sickness: Williams sits at 6,766 feet (2,062 m); stay hydrated and limit alcohol on your first day.
  • Grand Canyon parking: If driving to the canyon instead of taking the train, arrive before 9 AM to secure a parking spot.
  • Interesting facts
  • Williams was the very last town on historic Route 66 to be bypassed by Interstate 40 in 1984.
  • The town is named after 'Old Bill' Williams, a famous 19th-century mountain man, trapper, and guide.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Tusayan

stop · US

The ultimate basecamp for the Grand Canyon South Rim, Tusayan sits just outside the park gates, offering instant access to jaw-dropping canyon vistas, historic Native American culture, and hearty high-desert dining.

Stay: $150-280/n main strip — cheaper in Valle $90-140/n

Try: Navajo tacos, prickly pear margaritas, mesquite-smoked beef brisket, bison burgers

  • Grand Canyon National Park Vehicle Pass ~$35 · Valid for 7 days; buy online to skip the main entrance gate lines.
  • Grand Canyon Helicopter Tour ~$220 · Book weeks in advance, especially for highly coveted sunset slots.
  • Where to eat
  • Big E Steakhouse & Saloon — Juicy ribeye steaks, prime rib, and local craft beers in a massive, western-themed saloon. ~$35-60/pp
  • Canyon Star Saloon — Smoked BBQ ribs, pulled pork, and half-pound burgers accompanied by live country music. ~$25-45/pp
  • In & around
  • Grand Canyon South Rim — 10 min drive, iconic vistas and trailheads like Bright Angel
  • Desert View Watchtower — 35 min drive, historic stone tower with panoramic canyon and river views
  • Little Colorado River Gorge — 50 min drive, dramatic deep canyon overlook on Navajo Nation land
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Park entrance lines can exceed an hour mid-day; use the free Tusayan shuttle (purple route) to bypass the gate in summer.
  • Tusayan sits at 6,600 feet elevation; drink plenty of water and pack layers as temperatures drop rapidly after sunset.
  • Gas and groceries are highly marked up; fill up your tank and stock up on supplies in Flagstaff or Williams.
  • Interesting facts
  • Tusayan is Arizona's youngest incorporated municipality, officially gaining town status in 2010.
  • The town's name is derived from a Hopi term historically used by Spanish explorers to describe the regional Pueblo province.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Springdale

stop · US

Springdale is the dramatic red-rock gateway to Zion National Park, offering towering sandstone cliffs right outside your hotel door and a vibrant base for canyon hiking.

Stay: $180-350/n near canyon entrance — cheaper in Hurricane $90-150/n

Try: Bison burgers, Utah scones with honey butter, local craft beers

  • Angels Landing Seasonal Permit ~$6 · Online seasonal lottery required months in advance; day-before lottery also available.
  • Zion National Park Weekly Pass ~$35 · Valid for 7 consecutive days; America the Beautiful annual pass is also accepted.
  • Where to eat
  • Bit & Spur Restaurant & Saloon — Vibrant Southwestern spot serving excellent ribeye steaks and sweet chili glazed pork ribs (~$30-50/pp).
  • Oscar's Cafe — Casual local favorite famous for massive portions, green chili burgers, and craft beers (~$15-30/pp).
  • Camp Outpost Co. — Fast-casual eatery featuring wood-fired rotisserie meats, artisanal burgers, and outdoor patio dining (~$20-35/pp).
  • In & around
  • Grafton Ghost Town — 20 min drive, remarkably preserved 19th-century Mormon pioneer settlement
  • Kolob Canyons — 50 min drive, quieter red-rock finger canyons with a spectacular scenic drive
  • Sand Hollow State Park — 40 min drive, warm orange sand dunes meeting blue reservoir waters
  • Notes — read before you go
  • From spring to fall, private vehicles are banned on Zion Canyon Scenic Drive; you must use the free park shuttle.
  • Springdale parking lots and the park visitor center spaces often fill completely by 8:00 AM.
  • Always check the flash flood rating at the visitor center before entering narrow canyons like The Narrows.
  • Interesting facts
  • Springdale was originally settled in 1862 by Mormon pioneers who established orchards and communal farms.
  • The town is completely hemmed in by Zion's towering cliffs, offering dramatic 360-degree canyon views from any street.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Mammoth Lakes

stop · US

Nestled in the dramatic Eastern Sierra, Mammoth Lakes offers a spectacular base of volcanic landscapes, pristine alpine waters, and easy access to ancient ghost towns.

Stay: $180-300/n Village center — cheaper in Crowley Lake or June Lake $120-180

Try: Tri-tip BBQ, local Sierra Nevada trout, craft beers from Mammoth Brewing Company

  • Devils Postpile National Monument Shuttle ~$15 · Mandatory shuttle from Mammoth Mountain Main Lodge during summer peak hours to access the monument.
  • Bodie State Historic Park Admission ~$8 · Pay at the entrance kiosk; cash is highly recommended due to spotty credit card connectivity.
  • Where to eat
  • The Mogul Restaurant — Famous for charbroiled steaks, prime rib, and their unique table-side salad service (~$40-60/pp).
  • Burgers Restaurant — Juicy gourmet beef and buffalo burgers served in a casual, rustic cabin setting (~$15-25/pp).
  • Distant Brewing — House-smoked BBQ pulled pork sandwiches, soft pretzels, and locally brewed craft beers (~$20-30/pp).
  • In & around
  • Mono Lake Tufa State Natural Reserve — 30 min drive, otherworldly limestone towers rising from an ancient saline lake.
  • Bodie State Historic Park — 1 hour drive, a remarkably preserved, authentic gold-mining ghost town frozen in time.
  • June Lake Loop — 25 min drive, a scenic mountain loop passing four stunning alpine lakes with dramatic backdrops.
  • Notes — read before you go
  • High altitude warning: Mammoth Lakes sits at 7,880 feet (2,400m); stay hydrated and pace yourself to avoid altitude sickness.
  • Bear safety: You are in active black bear country; never leave food, trash, or scented items in your car.
  • Seasonal road closures: Tioga Pass (to Yosemite) and Reds Meadow Road (to Devils Postpile) are closed during winter and spring due to heavy snow.
  • Interesting facts
  • Mammoth Mountain is actually a dormant volcanic dome complex that last erupted about 57,000 years ago.
  • The area is a premier high-altitude training ground for Olympic long-distance runners and elite athletes.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Yosemite Valley

stop · US

Witness the sheer scale of towering granite monoliths like El Capitan and Half Dome, framed by roaring waterfalls in a glacier-carved valley.

Stay: $250-450/n inside the valley — cheaper in El Portal or Mariposa $120-220/n

Try: Boysenberry pie, craft beers from Yosemite Sierra Trail, tri-tip BBQ sandwich

  • Yosemite National Park Entrance Pass ~$35 per vehicle · Peak-hours driving reservations are required on select dates during spring, summer, and fall.
  • Yosemite Valley Floor Tour ~$40 · A 2-hour guided open-air tram tour; highly recommended to book weeks in advance.
  • Where to eat
  • The Mountain Room — Upscale dining featuring prime rib, steaks, and sustainable seafood with views of Yosemite Falls (~$40-70/pp).
  • 1850 Restaurant and Brewery — Located in nearby Mariposa, offering gourmet burgers, ribeye steaks, and locally brewed craft beers (~$25-45/pp).
  • Charles Street Dinner House — A historic steakhouse in Mariposa serving classic prime rib, BBQ ribs, and hearty American sides (~$35-60/pp).
  • In & around
  • Glacier Point — 1 hour drive, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the entire valley and Half Dome.
  • Mariposa Grove — 1 hour drive, home to over 500 mature giant sequoias, including the Grizzly Giant.
  • Tuolumne Meadows — 1.5 hour drive, pristine alpine meadows along the scenic Tioga Road (seasonal).
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Bear-proof food lockers must be used at all trailheads; never leave food or scented items in your car.
  • Expect severe traffic delays and full parking lots in the valley between 9 AM and 4 PM during peak season.
  • Cell service is extremely limited; download offline maps and park guides before entering the park.
  • Interesting facts
  • Yosemite was central to the development of the national park concept, first protected by Abraham Lincoln in 1864.
  • The park's iconic granite cliffs, like El Capitan, were sculpted by glaciers over millions of years.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Groveland

stop · US

A historic Gold Rush town nestled in the Sierra Nevada foothills, Groveland serves as the scenic northern gateway to Yosemite National Park with a vibrant, old-west Main Street.

Stay: €140-230/n historic downtown — cheaper cabins/motels nearby €90-140

Try: Tri-tip BBQ, craft beers, classic bison burgers, Sierra Nevada trout

  • Yosemite National Park Reservation ~€32 per vehicle · Peak-hours driving reservations are required during busy summer weekends and holidays.
  • Where to eat
  • Iron Door Saloon — Hearty half-pound burgers, ribeye steaks, and cold draft beers in California's oldest continuously operating saloon (~€20-40/pp)
  • Hungry Bear BBQ — Slow-smoked brisket, pulled pork sandwiches, and classic BBQ sides in a casual outdoor setting (~€15-30/pp)
  • Provisions Taproom & Bourbon Bar — Gourmet burgers, artisan flatbreads, and an extensive selection of craft beers and bourbons (~€25-45/pp)
  • In & around
  • Yosemite Valley — 75 min drive, home to iconic granite monoliths like El Capitan and Half Dome
  • Hetch Hetchy Reservoir — 45 min drive, a quieter, stunning valley featuring a massive dam and scenic waterfalls
  • Rainbow Pool — 15 min drive, a historic natural swimming hole with a waterfall, perfect for a quick dip
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Cell service is highly unreliable in the surrounding canyons; download offline maps before leaving Groveland.
  • Fill up your gas tank in town; fuel prices rise dramatically as you get closer to or enter Yosemite.
  • Keep food and scented items locked in bear-proof canisters or out of sight in your vehicle to avoid attracting local black bears.
  • Interesting facts
  • The town was originally named 'First Garrote' in 1850, a grim reference to a hanging that took place there during the Gold Rush.
  • The Iron Door Saloon's historic stone walls were built in 1852 using solid granite blocks to protect against frequent town fires.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Lone Pine

stop · US

A dramatic Eastern Sierra hub nestled between Mount Whitney and the otherworldly Alabama Hills, famous as Hollywood's favorite rugged western backdrop.

Stay: $120-200/n near Main St — cheaper options along US-395 $90-120

Try: Tri-tip BBQ, classic western burgers, local craft beers

  • Museum of Western Film History ~$10 · Pay at the door, but check seasonal hours before visiting.
  • Where to eat
  • The Grill at Lone Pine — Juicy ribeye steaks, classic burgers, and local draft beers in a cozy, rustic setting. ~$25-45/pp
  • Alabama Hills Cafe & Bakery — Famous for massive breakfast plates, homemade biscuits, and excellent lunch burgers. ~$15-25/pp
  • In & around
  • Alabama Hills — 10 min drive, bizarre rounded rock formations and iconic Movie Road
  • Manzanar National Historic Site — 15 min drive, poignant WWII Japanese-American internment camp museum
  • Whitney Portal — 20 min drive, dramatic mountain road leading to the Mount Whitney trailhead and a rushing waterfall
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Summer temperatures in the Alabama Hills can easily exceed 100°F (38°C); do your sightseeing early in the morning.
  • Cell service is highly unreliable off the main highway; download offline maps of the Alabama Hills and surrounding roads.
  • Watch your step around rock piles, as rattlesnakes are common in the desert brush during warmer months.
  • Interesting facts
  • Over 400 films and TV shows have been shot in the Alabama Hills, including 'Iron Man', 'Gladiator', and countless classic Westerns.
  • Lone Pine sits in the shadow of Mount Whitney, the highest peak in the contiguous United States at 14,505 feet.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details

More photos on Google Images →

Kernville

stop · US

Nestled along the wild Kern River, Kernville is a historic gold-rush town offering a rugged gateway to the Southern Sierra Nevada, giant sequoias, and hearty Western-style dining.

Stay: €110-180/n riverside cabins — cheaper motels near Hwy 178 €70-110

Try: Kern River Brewing craft beers, hickory-smoked tri-tip, classic Western burgers

  • Kern River Whitewater Rafting with Sierra South ~€65 · Pre-booking is highly recommended for weekend trips from May to September.
  • Where to eat
  • Kern River Brewing Company — Class V Stout Burger paired with locally brewed award-winning beers — ~€15-25/pp
  • Ewing's on the Kern — Hand-cut ribeye steaks and craft cocktails served on a deck overlooking the river — ~€30-50/pp
  • Johnny McNally's Fairview Oasis — Legendary 40-ounce Porterhouse steak in a rustic, historic canyon setting — ~€35-60/pp
  • In & around
  • Trail of 100 Giants — 1 hour drive, accessible boardwalk trail through massive ancient sequoias
  • Lake Isabella — 10 min drive, popular spot for windsurfing, fishing, and lakeside views
  • Keysville Historic Mining District — 15 min drive, semi-ghost town with 1850s gold rush ruins
  • Notes — read before you go
  • Cell service is extremely spotty or non-existent once you leave the town center; download offline maps beforehand.
  • The Kern River has extremely swift, dangerous currents; never swim outside designated safe areas.
  • Summer temperatures regularly exceed 35°C (95°F); carry extra water and watch for rattlesnakes on trails.
  • Interesting facts
  • Originally named Whiskey Flat, the town was renamed Kernville in 1864 to sound more respectable.
  • The original townsite of Kernville was completely submerged under Lake Isabella when the dam was built in 1953.
  • Kernville has served as a filming location for numerous classic Western movies starring Gene Autry and John Wayne.

⭐ must-see · 🏖 beach · 🍴 where to eat · sight — click a pin for details
